title: No Kill Switches in Cars Act
short_title: No Kill Switches in Cars Act
plain_summary: This bill cancels a rule that would have required car companies to put special technology in new cars to stop drunk or impaired people from driving. The technology would check if drivers are drunk or not driving safely and then prevent the car from starting or being driven. Right now, cars do not have to have this technology. The bill removes the requirement so car companies will not be forced to add this safety technology to vehicles.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the No Kill Switches in Cars Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Advanced Impaired Driving Technology
sections.text: Section twenty-four thousand two hundred twenty of the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act is completely removed and no longer exists as law. The Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act was passed as Public Law one hundred seventeen dash fifty-eight.
